name: Otis Spann
abstract: Otis Spann (March 21, 1924, or 1930 – April 24, 1970) was an American blues musician many consider the leading postwar Chicago blues pianist.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Muddy Waters and His Blues Band

about_quotes.speaker: Nat Hentoff
about_quotes.evidence: I recorded Otis Spann once, who was to me one of the greatest things that I ever had anything to do with. All you had to do was show him where the piano was and just stand back. And he was singing and playing. And afterwards I interviewed him for the liner notes
about_quotes.doc_title: Nat Hentoff interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007
